factors.name: Treasury Yield Spike & Real Rate Shock
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield surged to 5.19%, highest since pre-2008 financial crisis (per MarketWatch/Bloomberg). This aggressive long-end repricing is rate-negative for dividend-heavy Dow names (utilities, REITs, dividend payers) and signals market pricing of sustained inflation + potential Fed hikes.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Pivot Signals
factors.summary: Philadelphia Fed President Paulson stated rate hike is 'on table' if growth surges or inflation risks emerge, and ECB's Nagel/Kocher flagged June rate hikes unavoidable if Hormuz closure persists. This reversal from 'hold' to 'hike possible' is a sentiment inflection for equity valuations.

factors.name: Geopolitical Oil Premium (Iran/Hormuz)
factors.summary: Hormuz Strait tensions persist with little progress on US-Iran talks; oil priced in Hormuz risk premium (WTI steady but vulnerable). Higher energy costs threaten Dow industrials' input expenses and consumer discretionary demand (Home Depot already signaling caution per MarketWatch).

factors.name: Tech & Small-Cap Selloff Spillover
factors.summary: NASDAQ fell -0.84%, Russell 2000 -1.01% on 2026-05-19; S&P 500 -0.67%. Tech weakness in a yield-spike environment often precedes broader blue-chip pressure as growth expectations compress. Dow lagging tech but still down -0.37% in 24h, suggesting contagion risk.

factors.name: Global Bond Selloff & Carry Unwind
factors.summary: Major block sales in Treasury futures triggered 'capitulation' selloff across $31T market (Bloomberg). Japan's 20-year bond sale looming; Nikkei weakness signals Asia contagion. Dow's ~50% international revenue exposed to yen strength + broader carry deleveraging.

factors.name: USD Strength (Mixed Signal)
factors.summary: DXY rallying on strong ADP jobs data (+42,250 avg private payrolls, strongest since Oct 2025) and Trump Iran rhetoric. Stronger dollar is headwind for Dow multinationals' earnings translation but supportive of equities in deflation scenarios — partial offset.

factors.name: Inflation Persistence & Input Cost Risk
factors.summary: Oil servicemen, gasoline price spikes, mortgage rates jumped to highest since July (per MarketWatch). Toll Brothers beat but margin pressure evident. ECB explicitly warned prolonged Hormuz closure = material eurozone inflation. Input cost squeeze threatens Dow industrials (CAT, GS, JPM) margins.

factors.name: Earnings Resilience (Partial Support)
factors.summary: Toll Brothers upside, Home Depot guidance intact, Elanco/RB insiders buying (bullish signal). However, earnings beats are isolated; broader forward guidance from CAT, DE, BA not yet in view. Insufficient to offset macro headwinds.

factors.name: Valuation at Risk in Higher Yield Regime
factors.summary: Dow is 16-17x forward P/E; at 5.19% long-end yields, risk-free rate alone pressures dividend discount models. Dow components (JNJ, KO, PG) trading on yield support; yield spike = valuation compression.

factors.name: Credit Spreads & Financial Stress Signals
factors.summary: No explicit credit spread widening reported, but large Dow financials (GS, JPM, BAC) face margin compression in lower-carry, higher-volatility environment. Fed rate-hike talk = potential NII boost, but near-term uncertainty dominates.

screening.narrative: The Dow opened the week on a soft note as Treasury yields surged to crisis-era highs (5.19% on 30-year), triggering a broader risk-off cascade across equities and carry trades. Fed speakers (Paulson, ECB's Nagel/Kocher) signaled hawkish pivots if inflation persists or geopolitical shocks (Iran/Hormuz) extend, inverting market expectations from 'hold' to 'hike possible.' Meanwhile, oil remains elevated on unresolved Iran tensions, creating input cost headwinds for Dow industrials. Tech and small caps have already rolled over (-0.84% and -1.01% respectively), and while Dow has held better so far (-0.37% in 24h), its dividend and financials exposure leaves it vulnerable to further yield repricing. The move is in early-to-mid phase; downside momentum building, but pullback likely before fresh lows.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision (June 11) — if Hormuz still closed, ECB likely hikes, ripple to USD and Dow earnings translation
US CPI/PCE prints (late May/early June) — inflation data will either validate hawkish Fed pivot or ease pressure
FOMC next meeting (June) — Paulson hawkish tone may presage hike odds rising materially; market repricing risk
Oil price action & Hormuz resolution — any escalation = new energy shock; any truce = risk-on relief
Dow heavyweight earnings (GS, JPM, UNH, CAT, BA guidance) — input cost/margin pressure critical to watch
Treasury auction calendar (10Y, 30Y over next week) — whether yields stabilize or spike further
screening.risk_warning: Primary invalidation: rapid de-escalation in Iran tensions or credible Hormuz reopening would trigger immediate risk-on reversal, oil selloff, and yield pull-back, lifting Dow sharply. Secondary: if Fed speakers (Paulson, others) backtrack and signal 'hold-patient' tone, or if upcoming CPI comes cool (sub-2% core), hawkish bias collapses and Dow bounces hard. Tertiary: strong Dow heavyweight earnings beats + buyback support could stabilize / rally index despite macro headwinds. Monitor intraday 49,000 support; break below = 48,500-48,000 test likely.
screening.entry_guidance: Current move down is in PHASE 2 (underway ~3-6h); avoid chasing shorts into intraday weakness. Wait for retest of 49,300-49,500 resistance or pullback failure from support cluster at 49,000, then consider fresh short entry with stop at 49,700. Alternatively, if yield momentum pauses or Hormuz headlines soften, watch for reversal candles near 49,000 support for tactical long into retest of 49,500.
